supply_chain.dependencies: Third-party hotel managers responsible for all day-to-day operations; approximately 60% of hotels managed or franchised by Marriott International; 17% by Hyatt
supply_chain.risks: Dependence on third-party managers for operations; concentration risk with Marriott (60% of revenues); manager performance failures; conflicts of interest as managers operate competing hotels; inability to directly implement strategic decisions

regulatory_risks: REIT qualification requirements - must distribute 90% of taxable income annually
Federal and state corporate income tax risks if REIT status lost
Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) compliance requirements
Building codes and fire/life safety regulations
Environmental laws and regulations (hazardous substances, toxic materials)
State and local tax laws and property tax assessments
Labor laws and workplace regulations affecting hotel employees
New laws requiring hotel owners be deemed employer of workers
Potential joint employer determinations under new legislation
Foreign tax laws in Brazil, Canada, and India operations
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and anti-corruption laws
Data privacy and cybersecurity regulations (California Consumer Privacy Act and similar state laws)
Income tax law changes affecting REIT taxation
Changes to trade policy and tariffs
Government spending reductions and austerity measures

sanction_risks: Trade tensions and proposed tariffs between United States and trading partners such as China
International conflicts and geopolitical developments affecting global travel
U.S. immigration policies and border closings impacting international travel
